commit.message:
docs: add RF-DETR 1.6.0 release demo notebook (#824)

- Introduced a comprehensive example notebook, `release-demo_1-6.ipynb`, demonstrating key features of the RF-DETR 1.6.0 release.
- Covers end-to-end training workflow with PyTorch Lightning building blocks (`RFDETRModelModule`, `RFDETRDataModule`, `build_trainer`).
- Includes phase-wise training, dataset setup, metric visualization, and inference examples.
- Provides links to advanced training options, logger integrations, and export documentation.
- Bumped `transformers` version in `pyproject.toml` to `>=5.1.0` for compatibility.
- Refined `release-demo_1-6.ipynb` with improved descriptions, better organization, and enhanced examples:
  - Updated training phase details, dataset setup, and augmentation visualizations.
  - Expanded inference section with single-image and batch scenarios.
  - Added explanations for `trainer.predict()` pipeline and annotated results.
- Added `%matplotlib inline` to streamline plotting behavior.
- Adjusted `cpu().numpy()` calls to specific data types (`float`, `long`) in `release-demo_1-6.ipynb` for consistency.
- Improved import organization in both notebooks.
- Ensured consistent placement of `%matplotlib inline` for improved plotting behavior.
- Refactored import order for better readability and organization.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Harden chat index persistence

Prevent stale in-memory chat metadata from overwriting newer persisted chat index entries by tracking dirty and deleted chats during index saves.

Add regression coverage for persisted-vs-local merge scenarios around recovered shutdown state.

commit.message:
Streamline pipeline: remove params.outdir overrides and consolidate shared processes

Task 1: Remove module-level params.outdir overrides from main.nf and all
affected module files (canoes, clamms, cnvkit, gatk-gcnv, evaluate,
feature-extraction, normalise, survivor, train, truvari). Also removed
unused outdir.mkdir() calls from main.nf and modules-evaluate.nf.

Task 2: Create modules/modules-common.nf with shared BGZIP_SORT_INDEX_VCF
and NORMALISE_CNV_QUALITY_SCORES processes parametrised by tool_annot/
caller_name and dir_suffix vals.

Task 3 (partial): Updated modules-canoes, modules-clamms, modules-xhmm,
modules-cnvkit, modules-indelible, and modules-gatk-gcnv to include from
modules-common.nf, remove duplicate process blocks, and pass the correct
caller/dir_suffix arguments in workflow calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add CronJob support for get_logs resource resolution

CronJobs don't have spec.selector.matchLabels like Deployments/Jobs.
Resolve CronJob → most recent owned Job → pod label selector.

- Add 'cronjob' to supportedResourceKinds in logs_resolve.go
- Add resolveCronJobToLabelSelector: lists Jobs, filters by
  ownerReference matching CronJob UID, picks newest Job
- Extract extractMatchLabels helper from inline code
- Update tool description to list CronJob as supported
- Add batch/v1 (jobs, cronjobs) to test fake client & discovery
- Unit tests: 100% coverage on all logs_resolve.go functions
  (CronJob happy path, short name, no jobs, GVR error, list error,
   extractMatchLabels error branches)
- E2E test: CronJob resource resolution with full name and short name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use PrepareForBuildDependsOn to ensure wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js is built before static web asset discovery

The Razor SDK's static web asset pipeline requires the wwwroot/ file to exist
before ResolveProjectStaticWebAssets and ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs
run. PrepareForBuildDependsOn runs our BuildNpmBundle target early enough (before
any build targets evaluate @(Content)) to satisfy this requirement.

Also use a relative Content item path ('wwwroot\cgscript-cm6.js') instead of an
absolute path so ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs correctly picks it up
(it filters @(Content) by identity starting with 'wwwroot/').

This restores the staticwebassets/ and build/*.props structure in the nupkg that
was present in v2.20.1 but lost when wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js was gitignored.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: desktop mode with electron-mcp RPC/MCP on port 18101

Architecture:
  cicy-code --desktop
    → Go API server (port 18008)
    → electron (official binary) + electron-mcp JS (port 18101)
    → Electron window loads http://localhost:18008/?token=xxx

Key design: uses official Electron binary (npm i -g electron),
no code signing needed. electron-mcp provides RPC/MCP server
with 50+ tools (screenshot, automation, clipboard, CDP, etc).

Go backend:
  - desktop.go: ensureDesktop(), proxy /api/desktop/* → 18101
  - /api/desktop/status: desktop mode info + tool list
  - /api/desktop/rpc/*: proxy to electron-mcp RPC endpoints
  - /api/desktop/mcp: proxy to MCP SSE endpoint

npm bin:
  - --desktop flag: start API → wait health → launch electron
  - Discovery: global electron + global cicy → bundled desktop/
  - Auto-install electron and cicy if missing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update desktop submodule to 7ef8161

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add --desktop mode with electron-mcp submodule

- Add electron-mcp as git submodule at desktop/
- npx cicy-code --desktop: start API → wait ready → launch Electron
- Electron opens http://localhost:18008/?token=xxx via --url flag
- Discovery order: global 'cicy' CLI → bundled desktop/ → npx cicy
- Electron exit kills server, clean shutdown
- Add --desktop to local-deploy.md command table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set TERM=xterm-256color on PTY child process

Root cause: exec.Command inherits parent process TERM which may be
empty/dumb when started via nohup, causing 'terminal does not support
clear' in browser terminal.

Fix: explicitly set TERM=xterm-256color in cmd.Env before pty.Start().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Likely part of a cleanup or relocating/removing the UltraDynamicSky system from the repository.

feat: add 12 progressive Terraform IaC training labs

Created LABS/ directory with comprehensive hands-on training labs:

- LAB-01: Terraform basics — init/plan/apply/destroy workflow with local_file
- LAB-02: Variables, outputs, locals — types, validation, tfvars, precedence
- LAB-03: Multiple resources — implicit/explicit dependencies, lifecycle meta-args
- LAB-04: Data sources — query existing infra, AWS AMI/AZ/VPC examples
- LAB-05: Modules — structure, local/registry/git sources, versioning
- LAB-06: State management — remote S3+DynamoDB backend, state commands
- LAB-07: Count & for_each — dynamic resources, index vs key addressing
- LAB-08: Provisioners & null_resource — local-exec, remote-exec, triggers
- LAB-09: Workspaces — multi-env management, workspace-specific configs
- LAB-10: Import — CLI import, declarative import block, -generate-config-out
- LAB-11: Testing — validate, fmt, tflint, checkov, tfsec, terratest
- LAB-12: CI/CD — GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Terraform Cloud, Atlantis

Each lab includes: learning objectives, step-by-step instructions,
working HCL/YAML code examples, expected outputs, verification checklists,
cleanup instructions, 3 challenges, and solution hints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive Terraform IaC training materials

- README.md: Complete course overview with objectives, prerequisites,
  repository structure, learning paths, and navigation guide
- CONCEPTS.md: 18 deep-dive sections covering every Terraform concept
  with 3-5 practical examples each (IaC fundamentals, HCL syntax,
  providers, resources, variables, state, modules, dependencies,
  count/for_each, dynamic blocks, provisioners, workspaces, backends,
  Terraform Cloud, best practices, testing, security)
- COMMANDS.md: Full CLI reference for every command with 5-10 examples,
  flags, expected output, and environment variables reference
- QUICK-START.md: 10-step hands-on lab using local provider (no cloud
  account required), complete with expected outputs
- SETUP.md: Full environment setup for Linux/macOS/Windows including
  AWS/GCP/Azure credentials, S3/GCS backends, Terraform Cloud
- TOOLS.md: Required and recommended tooling (tflint, terraform-docs,
  checkov, tfsec, terrascan, Infracost, tfenv, VS Code, pre-commit)
- RESOURCES.md: Curated books, courses, YouTube channels, community
  modules, best practices guides, and certification info
- TROUBLESHOOTING.md: Common issues with root causes and step-by-step
  solutions including rollback strategies
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Contribution guidelines, development workflow,
  content standards, Terraform code standards, PR process
- LICENSE: MIT License (2024, ashad1990)
- .gitignore: Terraform-specific ignore rules
- .terraform-version: Pinned to 1.7.0 for tfenv/tfswitch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: create production-ready Express REST API from scratch

- TypeScript/Node.js Express API with PostgreSQL (pg) and Redis (ioredis)
- Feature-based architecture: auth, users, providers, appointments, catalogues
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens and Redis token blacklisting
- Zod validation middleware for request body/params/query
- Rate limiting via express-rate-limit (global + stricter auth limiter)
- Helmet + CORS security headers, Morgan HTTP logging
- Custom structured logger utility
- Standardized success/error/paginated response helpers
- Global error middleware handling Zod, JWT, and PostgreSQL errors
- Dynamic partial-update query builders (no ORM)
- Complete PostgreSQL migration with all tables, FKs, and indexes
- .env.example, .gitignore, tsconfig.json, package.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: adjust minimum Python version in broad charm compatibility tests (#2317)

A large number of our [broad charm compatibility
tests](https://github.com/canonical/operator/actions/workflows/published-charms-tests.yaml)
fail because the charm declares that it works with Python 3.8. That
conflicts with us pushing the latest ops 3.x into the dependencies.

Generally, if we've made a change to ops[...] that breaks charms, that
will be across Python versions, and we're more concerned with 3.10+
anyway (and in main, we're only supporting 3.10+). We could have some
compatibility tests that check the latest 2.x branch for charms that
expect 3.8, but that doesn't seem as useful.

This PR adjusts the dependency patching to also handle bumping the
Python version to 3.10. It [reduces the number of failures to around
6](https://github.com/tonyandrewmeyer/operator/actions/runs/23189775456),
some of which are only for static checks (which I haven't worked on in
this PR at all).

Since the changes are more complex now, they are moved out of the action
file (YAML+bash) and into a helper (Python) script. Primarily, we do the
work through tox, which I found to be the most consistent way to handle
things across packaging tools (particularly given that we are using
wheels now rather than git references).

The script also adds support for `tox.toml`, as some charms use that
rather than `tox.ini`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tonyandrewmeyer <826522+tonyandrewmeyer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Dima Tisnek <dimaqq@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Replace --ai-command flag with agent-mode subcommand (#300)

`--ai-command` was a top-level flag with no room for additional
configuration. This replaces it with a proper `agent-mode` subcommand
that adds an optional `--system-prompt` parameter.

### Changes

- **`src/lib.rs`**: Removes `ai_command` from `FlylineArgs`; adds
`AgentMode` to `Commands` with `--system-prompt` (optional) and
`--command` (required, multi-value)
- **`src/settings.rs`**: Adds `ai_system_prompt: Option<String>` to
`Settings`
- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: `start_ai_mode()` builds the final subprocess
argument as `"<system-prompt>\n<buffer>"` when a system prompt is
configured, falling back to the raw buffer otherwise

### Usage

```bash
# Equivalent to the old --ai-command
flyline agent-mode --command llm prompt

# With system prompt prepended to the buffer
flyline agent-mode --system-prompt "You are a bash expert. Suggest shell commands." --command llm prompt
```

agent-mode.
> It will have an optional field called --system-prompt. This will be a
string that is combined with the current command before passing it to
the subprocess.
> the next field is --command which takes in the rest of the args and
stores them just like what happens currently.</issue_description>

Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(training): make BestModelCallback save PTL-compatible checkpoints (#831)

Fixes two-phase training resume via trainer.fit(ckpt_path=...).

Previously, checkpoint_best_total.pth could not be loaded via PTL's
ckpt_path parameter because:
1. The file lacked the state_dict and loops keys PTL expects.
2. PyTorch >=2.6 defaulted torch.load to weights_only=True, rejecting
   the pickled TrainConfig Pydantic object stored under "args".

Source fix (no adapter needed):
- BestModelCallback now saves args as a plain dict (model_dump()) so
  weights_only=True works without registering safe globals.
- Adds PTL-compatible keys to every saved .pth: state_dict (model.
  prefixed), loops (epoch counter via _make_fit_loop_state), global_step,
  and pytorch-lightning_version.
- strip_checkpoint preserves these PTL keys so checkpoint_best_total.pth
  also works with ckpt_path.
- _make_fit_loop_state and _ckpt_args_get helpers moved/added to
  state_dict.py; readers (detr.py, module_model.py) use _ckpt_args_get
  to handle both dict (new) and namespace/object (legacy) args.
- checkpoint_io.py removed (no longer needed).

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use absolute positioning for copy button in code blocks (#684)

* fix: use absolute positioning for copy button in code blocks

The copy button was using `float: right` which caused it to take up
space in the document flow, resulting in an extra empty line appearing
at the bottom of code blocks.

Switch to `position: absolute` with `right: 1rem` and `bottom: 1rem`
to overlay the button without affecting the code block layout. This
also adds `position: relative` to `pre` elements to establish the
positioning context.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/doc-kit/pull/635

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add code block toolbar with language label and copy button

Replace the floating copy button with a dedicated toolbar at the bottom
of code blocks that includes a language label and copy button. This
resolves the visual issue where the copy button caused an empty-line
appearance at the bottom of code blocks.

Changes:
- Add createToolbarElement() to generate toolbar with language label
- Update copy button click handler to use closest('pre') for robust
  DOM traversal
- Add .code-toolbar flex layout and .code-language styling
- Update print media query to hide toolbar instead of copy button

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add optional path argument to --load-zsh-history (#298)

`--load-zsh-history` always read from `$HOME/.zsh_history` with no way
to specify a custom path.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`**: Replace `load_zsh_history: bool` with
`zsh_history_path: Option<String>` — `None` = disabled, `Some("")` =
default path, `Some(path)` = custom path
- **`lib.rs`**: Change CLI arg from a boolean flag to `Option<String>`
with `default_missing_value = ""`; update parsing to set
`zsh_history_path`
- **`history.rs`**: `parse_zsh_history()` now takes `Option<&str>`,
falling back to `$HOME/.zsh_history` when `None` or empty

## Usage

```bash
# Existing behavior unchanged
flyline --load-zsh-history

# New: load from a custom path
flyline --load-zsh-history /path/to/.zsh_history
```

feat(matter): use mquickjs by default (#180)

MicroQuickJS (aka. MQuickJS) is a JavaScript engine targeted at embedded
systems. It compiles and runs JavaScript programs using as little as 10
kB of RAM. The whole engine requires about 100 kB of ROM (ARM Thumb-2
code) including the C library. The speed is comparable to QuickJS.

MQuickJS only supports a subset of JavaScript close to ES5. It
implements a stricter mode where some error prone or inefficient
JavaScript constructs are forbidden.

Although MQuickJS shares much code with QuickJS, it internals are
different in order to consume less memory. In particular, it relies on a
tracing garbage collector, the VM does not use the CPU stack and strings
are stored in UTF-8.

This change adds a build-time option to use mquickjs (default) or
quickjs. Note that if using mquickjs matter.js support is not available.

The location of sbmd specs is now provided as a list of directories
instead of just one. This facilitates development and also splitting
spec directories by those with matterjs requirements and those without.



Refs: BARTON-357

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 5 SKILL.md files: finance (budget-planner, financial-analyst, invoice-generator) and education (tutor, quiz-creator)

Each file follows the required format with:
- Frontmatter: name, description, version, author, tags, license
- All 10 required sections in order
- At least 2 detailed ### Example subsections with real professional scenarios
- Quick Reference table with 6-7 rows
- Substantial Instructions (10 steps each)

commit.message:
Add KeyboardAwareForm component and document in UX guidelines (#29) (#31)

* Initial plan

* feat: create KeyboardAwareForm component and apply globally to all form screens

- Add KeyboardAwareForm as a form-specific wrapper around KeyboardAwareScrollView
  with CTA-friendly default bottom offset (24px)
- Add comprehensive test suite for KeyboardAwareForm (7 tests)
- Migrate all form screens to use KeyboardAwareForm:
  - sign-in, sign-up (auth)
  - onboarding profile
  - profile tab, network tab, announcements tab
  - conversation screen
  - player-sports-section feature
- Fix messages list screen: replace raw ScrollView with KeyboardAwareScrollView



* refactor: rename DEFAULT_FORM_EXTRA_BOTTOM_OFFSET to DEFAULT_EXTRA_BOTTOM_OFFSET



* docs: add KeyboardAwareForm usage guidance to mobile UX guidelines



---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GigiProve <15339926+GigiProve@users.noreply.github.com>

Disable horizontal swipe-back gesture on root and auth stacks (#28)

* Initial plan

* fix: disable horizontal swipe-back gesture on root and auth stacks

Add gestureEnabled: false to root layout and auth layout Stack
screenOptions to prevent unintended horizontal screen swiping.
The onboarding flow retains its own gesture settings (controlled
per-step via canGoBack).

commit.message:
feat(list): add allNamespaces flag to list_resources and get_events (#10) (#22)

Add `allNamespaces` boolean parameter to both `list_resources` and
`get_events` tools.

- allNamespaces=true lists resources/events across all namespaces by
  passing empty string as namespace to the API
- allNamespaces=true + explicit namespace returns a conflict tool error
- Tool descriptions updated to mention cross-namespace listing
- Unit tests added for both happy path and conflict error cases
- E2E tests added for allNamespaces listing and conflict validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(delete): add force flag to delete_resource (#11) (#21)

Add a force boolean parameter to delete_resource.

- force=true sets GracePeriodSeconds=0 for immediate deletion
- force=true + gracePeriodSeconds>0 returns a tool error (mutually exclusive)
- Elicitation prompt updated to warn about force delete side-effects
- Force delete still gated behind --allow-destructive

Unit tests cover: force path, conflict error, default behaviour, elicitation
E2E tests cover: force param accepted (dryRun), conflict validated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
